text.en_4: You have a bladder infection, also called a UTI.
text.en_6: You have a bladder infection, also called an uncomplicated UTI.
text.en_hl1: You have a bladder infection, called a UTI.

review.notes: Use only when lower UTI/cystitis pathway is appropriate.

text.en_4: Today we did not find signs of a kidney infection or a dangerous infection in your blood.
text.en_6: Today we did not find signs of a kidney infection, sepsis, or another emergency cause of your symptoms.
text.en_hl1: We did not see signs of a kidney infection or dangerous infection today.

review.notes: Requires chart-aware assembly if urine culture, pregnancy test, or imaging should be named.

text.en_4: Drink fluids if you are allowed to. Avoid alcohol or drinks that make burning worse until you feel better.
text.en_6: Drink fluids if you are allowed to. Avoid alcohol or drinks that worsen burning until symptoms improve.
text.en_hl1: Drink fluids if you can. Avoid drinks that make burning worse.

text.en_4: If a urine culture was sent, the hospital may call you if your antibiotic needs to change.
text.en_6: If a urine culture was sent, the hospital may contact you if the antibiotic needs to be changed.
text.en_hl1: If a urine culture was sent, we may call if your medicine needs to change.

review.notes: Should be modifier-gated when culture status is available.

text.en_4: Take the antibiotic exactly as prescribed. Finish it unless a doctor or pharmacist tells you to stop.
text.en_6: Take the antibiotic exactly as prescribed. Finish the course unless a doctor or pharmacist tells you to stop.
text.en_hl1: Take your antibiotic as told. Finish it unless a doctor tells you to stop.

review.notes: Antibiotic-specific side effects should be added by medication-aware assembly.

text.en_4: If you were given medicine for burning, use it only as directed. It may turn your urine bright orange.
text.en_6: If you were prescribed medicine for urinary burning, use it only as directed. It may turn your urine bright orange.
text.en_hl1: If you got medicine for burning, take it as told. Your pee may turn orange.

review.notes: Future medication-aware assembly should gate by phenazopyridine or local equivalent.

text.en_4: Come back now for fever, chills, back or side pain, vomiting, weakness, confusion, or feeling very sick.
text.en_6: Come back to the emergency department right away for fever, chills, back or side pain, vomiting, weakness, confusion, or feeling very sick.
text.en_hl1: Come back now for fever, chills, back pain, vomiting, confusion, or feeling very sick.

review.notes: Safety-critical pyelonephritis and sepsis return precautions.

text.en_4: Get checked again if burning or urgency is not starting to improve after 2 days of antibiotics, or if symptoms get worse.
text.en_6: Get checked again if burning or urgency is not starting to improve after 2 days of antibiotics, or if symptoms worsen.
text.en_hl1: Get checked if symptoms are not better after 2 days of antibiotics, or if they get worse.

review.notes: Treatment-failure warning. Time window needs local clinician approval.

text.en_4: See your regular doctor, clinic, or urgent care in 2 to 3 days if you are not getting better.
text.en_6: See your regular doctor, clinic, or urgent care in 2 to 3 days if symptoms are not improving.
text.en_hl1: See a doctor in 2 to 3 days if you do not feel better.

review.notes: Default outpatient follow-up for uncomplicated cystitis.
